name: Venison Sauerbraten
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the venison roast in a glass or earthenware bowl or baking dish with onions, bay leaves, peppercorns, berries, cloves, vinegar, and boiling water. Salt to taste.
2. Cover tightly and refrigerate for at least 3 days. Turn the meat twice a day; do not pierce it during turning.
3. Remove the venison from the marinade and reserve the mixture. Cook the venison in shortening in a heavy skillet until brown on all sides.
4. Add the reserved marinade to the skillet, return any onions that have fallen into the pan, and heat to boiling. Reduce the heat, then cover and simmer until the meat is tender (about 3-3½ hours).
5. Remove the venison and onions from the skillet; keep them warm.
6. Strain and measure the liquid in the skillet. Add enough water to reach a total of 2 1/2 cups. Return the strained liquid to the pan, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes.
7. Stir in the crushed gingersnaps and sugar; continue to simmer covered until just thickened (about 3 more minutes).
8. Serve the venison with onions and gravy. Baked potatoes and a citrus salad are recommended side dishes.
